description: Fix hardcoded timing delays and test coverage gaps in the E2E test suite that cause spurious failures under CI load. Specific issues: (1) hardcoded setTimeout waits in mcp-session-persistence, stdio-bridge, side-panel-theme, and security tests that are insufficient on slow systems; (2) analyze-site tab cleanup silently swallowing close failures; (3) plugin-logging buffer tolerance that is exceeded under high parallelism; (4) version-check-timing test missing the positive assertion that version check runs at startup.

userStories.title: Replace hardcoded 500ms delays in mcp-session-persistence.e2e.ts and stdio-bridge.e2e.ts
userStories.description: The 500ms hardcoded sleeps in openPersistentSseStream (mcp-session-persistence) and after notifications/initialized (stdio-bridge) are insufficient on slow CI systems and cause sporadic test failures. Replace with deterministic polling.
acceptanceCriteria: No `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 500))` remains in openPersistentSseStream in mcp-session-persistence.e2e.ts
The persistent SSE stream function polls for a signal that the stream is registered (e.g., a successful GET /mcp response or health check) rather than sleeping 500ms
No `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 500))` remains after the notifications/initialized send in stdio-bridge.e2e.ts
The stdio-bridge replacement waits for an observable state change rather than sleeping

userStories.title: Fix hardcoded 500ms delays in side-panel-theme.e2e.ts and security.e2e.ts overflow test
userStories.description: Replace hardcoded sleeps in side-panel-theme.e2e.ts (waiting for chrome.storage writes) and in the security.e2e.ts dispatch overflow test with deterministic polling.
acceptanceCriteria: No `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 500))` remains in side-panel-theme.e2e.ts for storage persistence waits
The theme persistence test polls for the persisted state rather than sleeping
No `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 2_000))` remains in security.e2e.ts as a dispatch synchronization mechanism
The security overflow test uses a deterministic wait (polling for dispatch count or health endpoint state) instead of a fixed sleep

userStories.title: Fix analyze-site.e2e.ts silent tab cleanup failure and plugin-logging.e2e.ts buffer tolerance flakiness
userStories.description: Two distinct test quality issues: (1) analyze-site tab cleanup silently swallows close errors via .catch(() => {}), masking isolation failures; (2) plugin-logging buffer assertion uses a narrow tolerance [4,6] that is exceeded under high Playwright parallelism, causing spurious failures.
acceptanceCriteria: In analyze-site.e2e.ts, browser_close_tab failures are logged rather than silently swallowed
The plugin-logging buffer size test does not have a tight upper-bound that is sensitive to parallel test activity
The plugin-logging test still verifies that at least 4 entries were emitted for the specific test action

userStories.title: Add missing startup assertion to version-check-timing.e2e.ts
userStories.description: The version-check-timing test only verifies that npm view does NOT appear after POST /reload. It never verifies that the version check actually ran at startup — the expected behavior it's named for. If startup version check is silently disabled, the test passes without detecting the regression.
acceptanceCriteria: version-check-timing.e2e.ts verifies that `npm view` appears in startup logs (before the reload)
The test still verifies that `npm view` does NOT appear in post-reload logs
The log snapshot approach (capturing log count before reload) is used to isolate pre- and post-reload logs
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/version-check-timing.e2e.ts

Context:
The test file has one test: 'POST /reload does NOT trigger npm view calls'. It:
1. Starts the MCP server
2. Waits for health
3. Records `logLengthBeforeReload = server.logs.length` (already present in the test)
4. Issues POST /reload
5. Checks that logs[logLengthBeforeReload:] do NOT contain 'npm view'

But it never checks that logs[0:logLengthBeforeReload] DO contain 'npm view' (the startup version check). If the startup version check is disabled, the test still passes.

Fix: Add an assertion before step 4 that verifies npm view ran at startup:
  // Verify version check ran at startup (logs before the reload)
  const startupLogs = server.logs.slice(0, logLengthBeforeReload);
  expect(startupLogs.some(l => l.includes('npm view'))).toBe(true);

This asserts the positive case: version check MUST run at startup. Add this immediately after the `logLengthBeforeReload = server.logs.length` line and before the POST /reload.

The variable name `logLengthBeforeReload` is already used in the test around line 67. The startup logs check uses this same variable to slice the logs array.

Constraints:
- Do NOT restructure the test — only add the startup assertion.
- The 'npm view' substring must match whatever the version check actually logs. Read the test more carefully to see if there are comments that indicate what log string to check for. If the existing test checks for 'npm view' in the negative assertion, use the same string in the positive assertion.
- If the server under test does not always have npm plugins to check versions for (no npm-discovered plugins in this test's config), this assertion may need to use a different log string. Read the test config setup to verify.

Important trap: The test starts the server with `localPlugins` only (no npm plugins, `OPENTABS_SKIP_NPM_DISCOVERY=1` set in startMcpServer). If version checking requires npm-discovered plugins, the assertion about 'npm view' appearing at startup would always fail. In that case, the test may need to be restructured to NOT skip npm discovery, or the assertion should check for a different log string that appears at startup version-check time even with no npm plugins.

Read the test fully to understand what config is used before adding the assertion. If the version check only runs when npm plugins are discovered, the test must first add an npm plugin to the config or the story cannot be implemented as stated — report back if this is the case.
